summary: Git detected dubious ownership of the repository directory `/work`, preventing git operations and causing the test runner to fail test discovery and execution with zero pattern matches.
root_cause: Git security check detected that the repository at `/work` is owned by a different user than the process running git commands, causing git to reject access with 'fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository'.

evidence.quote: fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'
evidence.reason: Git explicitly rejects access to the repository due to ownership mismatch

evidence.quote: failed to get git status for dirty hash: Git error: fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'
evidence.reason: Turbo build orchestrator also encounters the same git ownership error when trying to check repository status
Tests fail to execute with zero pattern matches, indicating the test environment is broken by the upstream git issue

evidence.quote: Failed to load test output Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open 'examples/with-jest/__tests__/index.test.tsx.results.json'
evidence.reason: Test results files are never created because tests never run, consequence of broken git state

suggested_fix: Configure git to treat `/work` as a safe directory by running: `git config --global --add safe.directory /work` before running tests

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
